q: An optical instrument used for viewing distant objects is called a ___.
Two types of telescope are ___ telescope and terrestrial telescope.
A refracting astronomical telescope consists of an objective of ___ focal length and an eyepiece of short focal length.
A refracting astronomical telescope consists of an eyepiece of ___ focal length.
In a refracting astronomical telescope, both lenses are ___ lenses.
___ invented a refracting telescope.
In an astronomical telescope, a real and inverted image of a distant object is formed at the focus of the ___.
The image formed at the focus of the objective in an astronomical telescope is ___ and inverted.
The image formed by the objective is within the ___ length of the eyepiece.
The final image formed by the eyepiece in an astronomical telescope is ___.
The final image formed by the eyepiece is virtual, larger than the first image and ___ with respect to the distant object.
The inverted final image in an astronomical telescope does not cause problems when viewing planets, ___, and moons.
The magnifying power of a refracting astronomical telescope is the ratio of the angle $\beta$ subtended by the ___ to the angle $\alpha$ subtended by the object.
For high magnifying power in a refracting astronomical telescope, the objective needs a ___ focal length.
For high magnifying power in a refracting astronomical telescope, the eyepiece needs a ___ focal length.
To obtain sharp images of distant objects, the objective of an astronomical telescope should have a ___ diameter.
A ___ mirror is often used as an objective instead of a large convex lens in astronomical telescopes.
It is difficult to make a perfect ___ lens of large diameter free from defects.
Most telescopes at astronomical observatories are ___ telescopes.
In a reflecting telescope, parallel rays from a distant object are reflected from the concave mirror to fall on a ___ mirror.
The eyepiece in a reflecting telescope acts as a ___ glass.
The astronomical telescope is not convenient for viewing objects on earth because the final image is ___.
To view terrestrial objects, an astronomical telescope must be modified to produce an ___ image.
In a terrestrial telescope, a ___ lens is used as an eyepiece to produce an erect image.
Terrestrial telescopes can be very ___ and inconvenient to use.
___ reflecting prisms are used in binoculars to reduce the length of terrestrial telescopes.
Total reflecting prisms are used in binoculars to ___ the length of the terrestrial telescope.
A ___ is an optical instrument consisting of a pair of telescopes.
Binoculars employ ___ reflecting prisms.
Successive total internal reflections in prisms greatly reduce the ___ of the telescope.
Binoculars are widely used as ___ telescopes.
